commands.review_summary:
The branch catches doctor runtime tool construction and schema-normalization failures for agent and bundled MCP tools, reports them as `core/doctor/runtime-tool-schemas` findings, and adds focused Vitest coverage.

PR surface: Source +74, Tests +141. Total +215 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main calls runtime tool construction and normalization inside the doctor schema check without converting thrown errors to findings, and the PR adds focused tests for those thrown paths.

target.title: fix(doctor): report runtime tool schema validation errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Converts structured doctor runtime tool-schema validation failures into actionable health findings instead of letting the check abort.
- Covers agent tool construction failures, provider/plugin schema normalization failures, and bundled MCP schema normalization failures while preserving MCP runtime cleanup.

## Verification

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (2 routed shards / 53 tests)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Touched-file scrub for the reported plugin/tool names plus private paths/network literals returned no matches.

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` runtime tool-schema health checks now report tool loading and schema normalization failures as doctor findings instead of aborting the schema validation check.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Codex worktree using the repo Vitest router for the touched doctor health-check surface.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.

Evidence after fix: 2 routed Vitest shards passed, covering 53 tests; oxfmt, diff check, touched-file scrub, and autoreview were clean.

Observed result after fix: simulated `fuzzplugin` tool construction, agent schema normalization, and bundled MCP schema normalization failures all return `core/doctor/runtime-tool-schemas` health findings and do not stop doctor from continuing its runtime schema pass.

What was not tested: no local Blacksmith/Testbox run was started beyond GitHub PR checks; no live provider/plugin secrets were used. GitHub PR checks on `ec876f1985c8` were successful after the PR-body refresh.
